programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews broaden their cultural horizons with a visit to the BBC in the company of a comatose window cleaner they think is Mrs Warboys cousin. Later, Victor makes a fresh start with a new job - but soon blots his copybook with a characteristic outburst. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor returns home from a disastrous shopping trip to discover he has been burgled, and an unwelcome wedding invitation compounds his misery. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Margaret is haunted by a nightmare in which she plans to murder her husband. Meanwhile, the Meldrews neighbour is busy preparing for his annual fete. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor tempts fate by agreeing to look after neighbours Patrick and Pippas house while theyre away - a situation made even more volatile by the arrival of his brother Alfred, who proceeds to rub him up the wrong way. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Richard Pearson

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret get stuck for hours in a bank holiday traffic jam, with miles of cars behind them, and nothing but the unwelcome sight of a horses backside in front. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: Beware the Trickster on the Roof
programme.desc: The Meldrew household falls foul of an evil curse, so Victor resolves to remove it. However, it soon becomes clear the task is easier said than done. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The adventurer sets off on a journey through the continent, beginning in the remote and little-visited north-east. In Venezuela, Simon meets some of the thousands of migrants fleeing the economic collapse of their country, while he learns that neighbouring Guyana could be on the verge of its own oil boom. He also meets a gun-toting former warlord who is now one of Surinames most successful businessmen, and ends his journey in French Guiana, where he goes to the launch site of the European Space Agency

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this series exploring extreme behaviour in the natural world, uncovering some of the strategies animals have developed to stay alive and to breed, from Komodo dragons tackling buffalo to the courtship battle of the humpback whale. He begins by focusing on the methods used by creatures such as cheetahs, bottle-nosed dolphins and Venus fly-traps in their fights for survival

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Clovelly in Devon, looking at its transformation from fishing harbour to romantic Victorian seaside resort. Thanks to a powerful and pioneering woman, Clovelly found itself at the forefront of the holiday revolution, and even today cars are banned and the village is privately owned, helping to preserve it as a reminder of a bygone age

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales continue their Swedish canal odyssey, following the ancient routes of the Vikings and travelling to Stockholm, where they navigate the citys canals. They later head out into the far-flung islands of the archipelago, before paying a visit to Drottningholm Palace Theatre, where they meet Wallander actor Krister Henriksson

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales travel down the Stratford-upon-Avon Canal, accompanied by Tims daughter and her family. The couple revisit places that hold fond memories from the early stages of their relationship, as well as looking back on the beginnings of their acting careers and putting on a performance in the church where Shakespeare is buried

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales venture into the open sea as they make their way through the islands of Scotlands coast. Their destination is the rarely used nine-mile Crinan canal on the Mull of Kintyre, described as the worlds most beautiful shortcut. However, the Atlantic waves off the coast of Mull force them to head inland to Tobermory. They later explore the holy island of Iona and the Isle of Jura

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore a network that is completely new to them as they embark on a voyage across the Netherlands. Their journey takes them to the gardens of Keukenhof and the Teylers Museum in Haarlem, before they arrive in Amsterdam, where they tour the capitals canal district - the Grachtengordel

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at vibrantly coloured monkeys in Venezuela, Costa Rica and West Africa. He encounters the red howler, which boasts one of the loudest calls in the wild, a capuchin that uses a tree as an anvil, the guenon, which communicates via facial expression, and the ground-dwelling gelada baboon

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the startling similarities between humans and great apes, meeting an orang-utan able to row boats and wash clothes and a gorilla that can communicate using sign language. He also traces the history of human settlement and considers the future of population control and food production

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this series exploring extreme behaviour in the natural world, uncovering some of the strategies animals have developed to stay alive and to breed, from Komodo dragons tackling buffalo to the courtship battle of the humpback whale. He begins by focusing on the methods used by creatures such as cheetahs, bottle-nosed dolphins and Venus fly-traps in their fights for survival

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how reptiles and amphibians, including the pebble toad and Jesus Christ lizard, conquer their shortcomings by using tricks and strategies to avoid predators. He also explores how chameleons can achieve a high level of accuracy with their muscle-propelled, extendible tongues, and finds out how a 9ft-long Komodo dragon can bring down a huge water buffalo

programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at vibrantly coloured monkeys in Venezuela, Costa Rica and West Africa. He encounters the red howler, which boasts one of the loudest calls in the wild, a capuchin that uses a tree as an anvil, the guenon, which communicates via facial expression, and the ground-dwelling gelada baboon

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the startling similarities between humans and great apes, meeting an orang-utan able to row boats and wash clothes and a gorilla that can communicate using sign language. He also traces the history of human settlement and considers the future of population control and food production

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this series exploring extreme behaviour in the natural world, uncovering some of the strategies animals have developed to stay alive and to breed, from Komodo dragons tackling buffalo to the courtship battle of the humpback whale. He begins by focusing on the methods used by creatures such as cheetahs, bottle-nosed dolphins and Venus fly-traps in their fights for survival

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: New World and Tina Charles perform on the vintage comedy and variety show, which includes sketches Patient, Wedding, Spoonerism and Hampton Wick

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at vibrantly coloured monkeys in Venezuela, Costa Rica and West Africa. He encounters the red howler, which boasts one of the loudest calls in the wild, a capuchin that uses a tree as an anvil, the guenon, which communicates via facial expression, and the ground-dwelling gelada baboon

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the startling similarities between humans and great apes, meeting an orang-utan able to row boats and wash clothes and a gorilla that can communicate using sign language. He also traces the history of human settlement and considers the future of population control and food production

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this series exploring extreme behaviour in the natural world, uncovering some of the strategies animals have developed to stay alive and to breed, from Komodo dragons tackling buffalo to the courtship battle of the humpback whale. He begins by focusing on the methods used by creatures such as cheetahs, bottle-nosed dolphins and Venus fly-traps in their fights for survival

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how reptiles and amphibians, including the pebble toad and Jesus Christ lizard, conquer their shortcomings by using tricks and strategies to avoid predators. He also explores how chameleons can achieve a high level of accuracy with their muscle-propelled, extendible tongues, and finds out how a 9ft-long Komodo dragon can bring down a huge water buffalo

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales continue their Swedish canal odyssey, following the ancient routes of the Vikings and travelling to Stockholm, where they navigate the citys canals. They later head out into the far-flung islands of the archipelago, before paying a visit to Drottningholm Palace Theatre, where they meet Wallander actor Krister Henriksson

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Roy Clarkes comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and David Jason. Tight-fisted shopkeeper Arkwright purchases fire-damaged stock, only to find all the tins have lost their labels

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: As Lucy and Guys relationship intensifies, Lee begins to feel isolated and is encouraged to try speed-dating. Tim takes Barbara on a night out with surprising consequences. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony returns from holiday sporting an ill-advised beard, which he mistakenly thinks is bound to impress Deborah. Gary, meanwhile, has a problem of a different kind - Dorothy has moved in and he is not so sure it is a good idea after all, especially once she starts filling the flat with girly things. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Cathy goes to extreme lengths to impress her psychologist boyfriend, much to Agness disgust, while Rory locates the perfect apartment for him and Dino - the problem is, its in Kilbride, meaning hell have to learn to drive. Mark prepares to emigrate to Australia. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted has a chance of stardom when he is hired as a late replacement at an uptown show for a fellow comedian. It is rumoured that talent scouts from the TV show Opportunity Knocks will be in the audience. Classic comedy following life at Maplins holiday camp, starring Paul Shane, Su Pollard and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: After a recent break-up, Alma tries to get her life back on track, but with no job, no qualifications and a rebellious streak a mile wide, it is not going to be easy. Meanwhile, her heroin-addicted mother has been sectioned for arson, and her grandmother Joan wants nothing to do with it. Comedy pilot, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison spends more time with Lucy and starts planning to make a big investment - but the Captain and Kitty uncover something suspicious, and need Fannys newfound sleuthing skills to get to the bottom of the mystery before it is too late. Thomas works on his football skills with a little help form Humphreys head, and Julian lends an unseen helping hand to Mikes promo film

programme.title: Toast of London
programme.desc: Steven needs to raise money to pay for an acrimonious divorce and there are two potential solutions - a starring role in a new film being made by an Arab billionaire, or a laxative advert. He plumps for the former - against the advice of his agent - but given the content of the movie, has the actor made the right decision? Comedy, starring Matt Berry

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Boycie reluctantly signs up for Farm Idol, a new reality TV show aimed at finding Britains sexiest rural workers, while Mrs Cakeworthy becomes suspicious of Marlene when a well-dressed man visits her while her husband is away. Comedy, starring John Challis and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: The Long Legs of the Law
programme.desc: Rodney starts going out with a policewoman, much to the consternation of dodgy dealer Del-Boy, who is terrified that one wrong word from his little brother could see him banged up for five years. Vintage comedy, starring Nicholas Lyndhurst, David Jason and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del gets the chance to sell off a couple of antique urns - the only problem being that one of them contains the ashes of Triggers late grandfather. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Lennard Pearce and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del takes up cards in his latest bid to make his first million - much to the annoyance of Rodney and Grandad, who reckon he will end up gambling away all their worldly goods. Comedy with the wheeler-dealing Trotter family, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy cannot believe his ears when Rodney tells him he has found a girlfriend. The trouble is, the woman is old enough to be his mother - and to make matters worse, her husband is about to be released from prison

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del jumps at the opportunity to decorate the local Chinese restaurant to make a quick profit, and the results are outstanding in more ways than one. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodney reluctantly agree to let Grandad accompany them on their holiday abroad - a decision they come to regret. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: After lending assistance to a wealthy woman in distress, Del seizes a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to improve his social standing by launching his own chandelier-cleaning business. As part of the venture, he ropes a reluctant Rodney and Grandad into helping him. David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst star

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses

programme.desc: With exciting news concerning the shows new presenting line-up, Alan and Jennie get their teeth into more of the pressing issues of the day. programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now more established as the shows co-host, Alan is able to produce the kind of brave, no-nonsense TV other broadcasters can only dream of. In this edition, he talks police dog bravery and product placement, before meeting his own celebrity lookalike. Steve Coogan stars, with Susannah Fielding

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to stop Madge from moving away and taking Mavis with her - but there is only one person who can change their minds, and he has been dead for years. Mrs Featherstone makes Mr Newbold an offer he cannot refuse, and Eric and Cyril vow to be more careful with their money

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary finally prepares to give up his freedom but fears he has not slept with enough women, while Dorothy worries that gherkins in the pub will be the only highlight of her special day. Meanwhile, best man Tony has an ulterior motive for making sure the unhappy couple get through their ordeal safely. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Quentin, Neil Morrissey and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Alma treats best friend Leanne to a shopping spree, during which she discovers Bolton Acting School and decides to join up. She also arranges another family meeting between Lin and Joan at an upmarket restaurant, but its not to everybodys taste. Comedy, starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Theres some good news for Lin as the hospital staff inform her that her section could finally be lifted, although this is dependent on her either moving into a hostel or in with a family member. As Alma is finally getting her life back on track and the hostel isnt exactly the right place for a recovering addict, Alma has to convince her grandma to let Lin move in with her - but will this be a step too far for Lin and Joan?

programme.desc: Sally and Millie are called to a domestic affray in which a man throws out his ex-heroin addict partner after finding drugs on the doormat. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Callum arrives at a crime scene expecting to arrest a woman accused of theft, but the suspect turns out to be a victim of domestic abuse who desperately needs help. Anxious to protect her, he is frustrated by her reluctance to bring charges against her husband and further thwarted when CID identify the man as an armed robber and put him under surveillance, leaving the woman vulnerable to his violent outbursts

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of six episodes about the problems of inner-city schools, a reluctant Nate is appointed as Safer Schools Officer at Deansgate Comprehensive following an arson attack on the art department. On his first day he breaks up a fight between two girls who clearly know who is responsible for the fire and Smithy is stabbed in the chest as he pursues the investigation. Convinced there is a job to be done at the school, and hoping to renew his acquaintance with attractive teacher Becky James, Nate volunteers to carry on in the liaison role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a Deansgate Comprehensive pupil collapses in the corridor, Safer Schools Officer Nate finds he has a drug problem on his hands. Trusted pupil Jimmy Ryan is caught in possession of a stash of Ecstasy forced on him by his older brother, who lives in fear of the local dealer, and when Jimmy is taken into care, accusing Nate of breaking his promises, the liaison officer realises what he has let himself in for

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenagers house is trashed in revenge for an alleged rape and Safer Schools Officer Nate is horrified to discover the extent of peer pressure, drinking, underage sex and bullying among the pupils of Deansgate Comprehensive. Allegations of rape at a booze-fuelled teenage party leave him wondering whether he will ever be able to make his mark in his new role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A hate campaign sparked by a rape case from 18 months previously divides pupils and parents at Deansgate Comprehensive, leading to schoolyard confrontations and classroom fights. Safer Schools Officer Nate discovers the victim of the slanderous campaign is entirely innocent of the crime, and the identity of the person spreading malicious lies comes as a terrible shock

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A break-in at Deansgate Comprehensive proves to be little more than a prank by two youngsters, but a school governor takes his daughter away, blaming the staff for her atypical behaviour when she confesses her part in the crime. Teacher Becky James fails to turn up for work and Nate finds her beaten unconscious at her home by someone who desperately wants to get hold of her laptop

programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows dont fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dogs neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats 15-stone English mastiff Bear, who has a ruptured ligament in his knee - but as the dog goes into theatre, a major team effort is needed to get this massive patient through surgery. Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car and golden retriever Henry is suffering from a chronically damaged spinal disc

programme.desc: South Central paramedics treat a pregnant woman who has fallen on her baby bump. Plus, paramedics are called to an unconscious child with a complex medical condition

Anxious to protect her, he is frustrated by her reluctance to bring charges against her husband and further thwarted when CID identify the man as an armed robber and put him under surveillance, leaving the woman vulnerable to his violent outbursts

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of six episodes about the problems of inner-city schools, a reluctant Nate is appointed as Safer Schools Officer at Deansgate Comprehensive following an arson attack on the art department. On his first day he breaks up a fight between two girls who clearly know who is responsible for the fire and Smithy is stabbed in the chest as he pursues the investigation. Convinced there is a job to be done at the school, and hoping to renew his acquaintance with attractive teacher Becky James, Nate volunteers to carry on in the liaison role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a Deansgate Comprehensive pupil collapses in the corridor, Safer Schools Officer Nate finds he has a drug problem on his hands. Trusted pupil Jimmy Ryan is caught in possession of a stash of Ecstasy forced on him by his older brother, who lives in fear of the local dealer, and when Jimmy is taken into care, accusing Nate of breaking his promises, the liaison officer realises what he has let himself in for

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenagers house is trashed in revenge for an alleged rape and Safer Schools Officer Nate is horrified to discover the extent of peer pressure, drinking, underage sex and bullying among the pupils of Deansgate Comprehensive. Allegations of rape at a booze-fuelled teenage party leave him wondering whether he will ever be able to make his mark in his new role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A hate campaign sparked by a rape case from 18 months previously divides pupils and parents at Deansgate Comprehensive, leading to schoolyard confrontations and classroom fights. Safer Schools Officer Nate discovers the victim of the slanderous campaign is entirely innocent of the crime, and the identity of the person spreading malicious lies comes as a terrible shock

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A break-in at Deansgate Comprehensive proves to be little more than a prank by two youngsters, but a school governor takes his daughter away, blaming the staff for her atypical behaviour when she confesses her part in the crime. Teacher Becky James fails to turn up for work and Nate finds her beaten unconscious at her home by someone who desperately wants to get hold of her laptop

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A skier falls over on a steep slope and suffers a suspected brain injury in Wakefield and a DIY enthusiasts attempts to fix the heating in his chilly home end in disaster

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads to a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: The Auckland Rescue Helicopter crew are dispatched to a critical situation when a drag racer smashes into a concrete wall

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: Four people are left fighting to keep their heads above water when their boat sinks in the Firth of Thames

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon to a big welcome from both their families. However, Smithy seems disconcerted by his pals return and Nessa makes a confession that spoils the happy occasion. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne, James Corden and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an Essex wife - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews

Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Kenzo embarks on a school project about his family, giving everyone the opportunity to look back at some of the crazier moments in the Harpers history

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The local council insists that Ben is disabled so he happily plays along with the mistake - until he comes to regret his actions. Comedy, guest starring Penelope Wilton

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michaels news comes as a bit of a shock to Ben, but Susan has more of a shock at Bens reaction, which causes more than its fair share of trouble

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben forgets about Valentines Day, but discovers Susan has a more attentive admirer - Martin, the benign elderly widower from next door, is besotted with her and has built a shrine to her in his kitchen. Comedy, guest starring Sam Kelly and Dan Renton Skinner

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey takes a fancy to an Australian backpacker, but a cantankerous Ben is unhappy about the situation and starts to worry when it looks as if Craig could be his illegitimate son

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows dont fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog is brought in after collapsing and struggling to move, and after a full medical assessment, Noel realises an exploded disc in the dogs neck is pressuring its spine to such an extent it is slowly causing paralysis. The vet also treats an excitable Staffordshire bull terrier suffering from a problem where the bones in its elbows do not fit properly and a cat that has been shot with an airgun

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats 15-stone English mastiff Bear, who has a ruptured ligament in his knee - but as the dog goes into theatre, a major team effort is needed to get this massive patient through surgery. Siamese cat Bluebell is rushed in after being hit by a car and golden retriever Henry is suffering from a chronically damaged spinal disc

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics treat a pregnant woman who has fallen on her baby bump. Plus, paramedics are called to an unconscious child with a complex medical condition

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics have their hands full with a high-speed motorcycle crash, a woman with abdominal pain and an old lady who has fallen, injuring her face

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Three-year-old Isaac needs an operation on his tongue, a woman has her toes straightened and part of a toe amputated, and a man has biopsies taken from his prostate

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Nigel has keyhole surgery to repair his hernia - but things go wrong when hes back on the ward. Carol has a pin removed from her ankle, which was previously fractured

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team head to Coolaney in Country Sligo to adapt the home of Claire Carpenter to her needs so she can return from hospital after suffering a stroke

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Scarborough to explore one of the greatest royal fortresses in England

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey in the East Yorkshire resort of Hornsea, famous for its distinctive pottery. In Hull, he discovers how one of Hull FCs star players made history as Britains first black rugby captain. From Hull, Michael hitches a ride on a fine-looking barge heading to Goole on a network of canals. At Spurn Point, Michael hears how along this coast an ingenious 19th-century RNLI invention saved many lives

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the towns history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Northern Irelands spectacular Causeway coast to begin a new journey, which takes him along the north and east coasts through Ulster and Leinster. He begins in Co Derry on the windswept shores of Loch Foyle, where the largest accumulation of coastal sands in Ireland has formed a system of dunes visible from space. He also learns how scientists are monitoring the site and hears how their work has been picked up by fellow scientists in the United States to help research the surface of Mars

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Peter wants a Triumph Herald Convertible, like the one he had when he first learned to drive. But now rare, will £9000 be enough to find a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Rupert has decided life is too short not to follow his dream of owning a Morris Traveller. Will Ruperts £13,000 budget stretch to a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Eric loves open-top motoring, but his sports car is too low and he needs an automatic car, so Sheldon and Peter suggest a VW Golf Cabriolet that might stretch Erics budget

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Norman has dreamt of owning a Volvo 850R since watching Volvo British touring cars race, but fixers Sheldon and Peter could struggle to bring Normans dream to life

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher takes on the role of agony aunt and ends up writing letters home for his fellow inmates - but he soon discovers domestic problems of his own when his daughter brings bad news on visiting day. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Patricia Brake

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber is feeling dejected after his romantic fortunes take a turn for the worse, so Fletch tries to revive his friends flagging spirits. However, his cunning plan has one potential drawback - it involves his own daughter. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simons journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypts southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The last leg of Simons journey through South America takes him from Chiles Atacama Desert right down to Tierra Del Fuego, the most southerly inhabited place on Earth. In the Atacama, one of the driest places on the planet with is as little as 1mm of rain per year, Simon discovers shocking evidence of human pollution

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the documentary exploring Earths natural history and the factors that have affected it. The first edition examines how the sun affects life on Earth through the seasons, following a polar bear preparing its cubs for a long journey to avoid becoming stranded on floating glaciers. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberias Lake Baikal, home to the worlds only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earths tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 thats been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930s Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine thats seen better days

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rusty 1970 Fiat 500 goes under the hammer - Derek Mathewson reckons its the kind of vehicle that is so iconic that it has to be saved

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle thats ripe for restoration

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the glens of Antrim, where he is transfixed by tales of fairies and legends of the sea. At the entrance to Belfast Lough in Whitehead he discovers a museum which tells the story of Irelands railway heritage. He also sees if he has what it takes to join the local rowing club. At the medieval Carrickfergus Castle, Michael learns of the towns important role in the history of Ireland, when William of Orange landed at its harbour and went on to fight the Battle of the Boyne, which is commemorated by Unionists to this day

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Bangor, County Down, en route to the Northern Irish capital of Belfast. Along the way he enjoys live music on a boat, stops off at the imposing parliament building at Stormont and explores the regenerated Titanic Quarter on the Maritime Mile

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey along the Atlantic and Irish Sea coasts of Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. This time, he makes his way from the vast beaches of the Carlingford Peninsula towards Drogheda and Newgrange, before arriving in capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, where he visits Dublin Castle and Dublin docks

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire. He joins the crew of the RV Malice to survey what lies beneath the waves in Dublin Bay. Back on dry land, the DART takes Michael to his next stop, Sandycove, where James Joyce, one of Irelands greatest writers, found inspiration

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germanys plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Bomber Command is unable to hit its targets precisely and attacks German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simons journey begins at the Giants Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwells influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out to explore the meandering waterways of inner city Birmingham including the Soho Loop and the Engine Arm Canal

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the worlds caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the worlds highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: The ever-optimistic Bob tries to broaden Terrys social circle by inviting him to a trendy dinner party, but as usual his well-meaning efforts result in disaster. Rodney Bewes and James Bolam star as the original men behaving badly

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Thelma looks on with disapproval as Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions to his new house, releasing a flood of nostalgic memories about their youthful misadventures. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the towns history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Northern Irelands spectacular Causeway coast to begin a new journey, which takes him along the north and east coasts through Ulster and Leinster. He begins in Co Derry on the windswept shores of Loch Foyle, where the largest accumulation of coastal sands in Ireland has formed a system of dunes visible from space. He also learns how scientists are monitoring the site and hears how their work has been picked up by fellow scientists in the United States to help research the surface of Mars

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Peter wants a Triumph Herald Convertible, like the one he had when he first learned to drive. But now rare, will £9000 be enough to find a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Rupert has decided life is too short not to follow his dream of owning a Morris Traveller. Will Ruperts £13,000 budget stretch to a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Eric loves open-top motoring, but his sports car is too low and he needs an automatic car, so Sheldon and Peter suggest a VW Golf Cabriolet that might stretch Erics budget

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Norman has dreamt of owning a Volvo 850R since watching Volvo British touring cars race, but fixers Sheldon and Peter could struggle to bring Normans dream to life

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher takes on the role of agony aunt and ends up writing letters home for his fellow inmates - but he soon discovers domestic problems of his own when his daughter brings bad news on visiting day. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Patricia Brake

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber is feeling dejected after his romantic fortunes take a turn for the worse, so Fletch tries to revive his friends flagging spirits. However, his cunning plan has one potential drawback - it involves his own daughter. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simons journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypts southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The last leg of Simons journey through South America takes him from Chiles Atacama Desert right down to Tierra Del Fuego, the most southerly inhabited place on Earth. In the Atacama, one of the driest places on the planet with is as little as 1mm of rain per year, Simon discovers shocking evidence of human pollution

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the documentary exploring Earths natural history and the factors that have affected it. The first edition examines how the sun affects life on Earth through the seasons, following a polar bear preparing its cubs for a long journey to avoid becoming stranded on floating glaciers. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberias Lake Baikal, home to the worlds only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earths tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: Train Truckers

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Wales to collect an Austin 7, and a gem of a Bentley, a British classic with coachwork built by top notch Parisian outfit Kellner who specialised in luxury

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the glens of Antrim, where he is transfixed by tales of fairies and legends of the sea. At the entrance to Belfast Lough in Whitehead he discovers a museum which tells the story of Irelands railway heritage. He also sees if he has what it takes to join the local rowing club. At the medieval Carrickfergus Castle, Michael learns of the towns important role in the history of Ireland, when William of Orange landed at its harbour and went on to fight the Battle of the Boyne, which is commemorated by Unionists to this day

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Bangor, County Down, en route to the Northern Irish capital of Belfast. Along the way he enjoys live music on a boat, stops off at the imposing parliament building at Stormont and explores the regenerated Titanic Quarter on the Maritime Mile

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway journey along the Atlantic and Irish Sea coasts of Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. This time, he makes his way from the vast beaches of the Carlingford Peninsula towards Drogheda and Newgrange, before arriving in capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, where he visits Dublin Castle and Dublin docks

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire. He joins the crew of the RV Malice to survey what lies beneath the waves in Dublin Bay. Back on dry land, the DART takes Michael to his next stop, Sandycove, where James Joyce, one of Irelands greatest writers, found inspiration

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germanys plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Bomber Command is unable to hit its targets precisely and attacks German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part one of two. Simon explores the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examines the rich culture and troubled past of the island. Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simons journey begins at the Giants Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwells influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming sets out to explore the meandering waterways of inner city Birmingham including the Soho Loop and the Engine Arm Canal

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the worlds caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the worlds highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Ormerod faces a taxing time when a prison van overturns on the moors. The driver is killed, a guard is seriously injured and one of the convicts escapes, while the others are rounded up and taken for treatment at the hospital, where Ken Hopkirk recognises an old friend. Goodwin fails to diagnose a serious case of post-natal depression - an error which almost ends in tragedy when the mother is found standing perilously close to a cliff edge - and student nurse Samantha Beaumont discovers she has an unexpected love rival. programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Ormerod faces a testing time when his father-in-law arrives demanding to know whether he was seeing Dr Weatherill while his late daughter was still alive. Following a heated exchange, it emerges that the disgruntled patriarch has hired a private detective to investigate the couple - but the situation only serves to bring them closer together, culminating in a marriage proposal. At the Royal, a painfully thin girl causes concern for Goodwin - whose green-eyed monster emerges when student nurse Samantha Beaumont agrees to a date with a younger man - and the hospital is evacuated after a suicide victims corpse is brought in emitting toxic fumes. Medical drama, starring Robert Daws, Amy Robbins, Paul Fox and Anna Madeley

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry looks at autumn in the northern hemisphere and spring in the south, seasons of change that bring opportunities, but also huge challenges. By September temperatures in northern Canada can plummet as low as -20C, but there is one stretch of river that still flows and bears are able to fish for the final salmon of the season. In southern Africa the rains have been gone for many months and watering holes have all-but dried up, while a king penguin chick on Marion Island answers the call of the ocean

programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the worlds fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour

programme.title: This Time Next Year

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in SOS: Survive or Surrender, hosted by former commando Jason Fox, who is in on the whole thing. Actor and comedian Guz Khan is the guest saboteur

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where six hopefuls compete for £1 million. However, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: Ireland v Italy (Kick-off 5.40pm). Coverage of both teams second match of the Championship, held at Dexcom Stadium. Ireland claimed a dominant 54-212 away win when the teams met in last years competition and finished two places above their opponents in the table in third, but Le Azzurre won 27-21 on Irish soil in 2024

programme.title: Virgin Media News Update
programme.desc: A round-up of the days national and international headlines

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: The auditions reach a conclusion as the final assortment of acts take to the stage in Blackpool hoping to impress judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. A coveted space in the semi-finals is at stake and with it the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance. Presented by Ant and Dec

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor Phil Dunster, who rose to fame in hit show Ted Lasso, to talk about his new comedy Rooster. Actress Cush Jumbo chats about the second season of crime thriller Criminal Record, and comedian Josh Widdicombe discusses his podcast The Museum of Pop Culture, while Dan Levy, creator, writer and star of the multi-award winning show Schitts Creek, discusses the series

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Robbie Savage and his son Charlie, Helen Flanagan and her mum Julia, and Tayce and her dad Qariem Radix compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi travels to the French Pyrenees, where ex-pats Mike and Joss invested everything they had into creating a holiday retreat and a beautiful family home for themselves and their two young boys. But despite the fabulous setting, the hotels bookings are poor and with their savings dwindling, the couple are desperate not to take another wrong turn. Can Alex get them back on the right track?

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Airbus A380, which as the largest passenger plane ever built can carry more than 850 people non-stop nearly half-way around the world. With the largest wingspan of any commercial aircraft and four specially developed mighty turbo jet engines, the A380 weighs 560 tonnes and getting it off the ground would not have been possible without the work of past innovators such as George Cayley and Frank Whittle. Key developments in aerospace engineering - from Richard Whitcombes energy-efficient wing-tip designs to Nasa engineers cutting-edge control system, Fly by Wire - helped bring the A380 to fruition

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir responds to an urgent call from a pedigree sheep breeder whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, and has to choose between a challenging amputation or euthanasia. Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a man who has used a wheelchair since a motorcycle accident, and who now vows to walk again. Also featured are a schoolgirl pursuing a record deal, a couple hoping to have a baby with the help of a surrogate, and a woman who was adopted as a child and is now hoping to track down her long-lost brother

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare classic festive dishes, including a fondue served with filo-wrapped asparagus and pancetta-covered potatoes. Lisa then shares her classic nut roast recipe, dressed up with a festive wreath, and John cooks cured salmon with aromatic spices, while for dessert the pair make hazelnut roulade with amaretto cream and a mulled wine compote

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: A technical hitch arises when an A380 lands, and six times the regular number of cleaners are needed to help meet a tight schedule ahead of take-off. A suspicious looking substance is spotted by security when a bag is scanned through cutting-edge technology thats being trialled at the airport, while Passenger Experience Manager Suki helps a taxi driver whos doubled over in pain with a trapped nerve

programme.title: Alex Polizzis Secret Italy
programme.desc: The presenter uncovers some of the countrys unknown delights, taking in Capri, Ischia, Salina and Filicudi

programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from Bognor Regis, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in The Perfect Pooch, a dog-themed challenge show hosted by Clare Balding, who is in on the whole thing. Former football manager Harry Redknapp is the guest saboteur

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lydia offers Moira time alone in the house and she looks forward to spending quality time with Cain. Jacob tells Ross about being bullied by Dr Todd and Belle is excited ahead of her date with Kammy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carl tries to get Theo arrested for assault by goading him into starting a fight. Kit arrives with an update about Megans case. Carla and Lisas hopes of a happy family future are dealt a devastating blow when the social worker calls

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Joe Swash, Joanna Page, and Angellica Bell join Stephen Mulhern as they try to say what they see and guess well-known phrases for their chance to win 50,000 pounds for charity

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny looks at what the future for men might look like. He meets stay-at-home dads in Newcastle, a sex therapist in Brighton, and goes alternative shopping with young DJ Woody Cook. He meets with psychologists at Sunderland University to try to understand the male mental health crisis, before observing a very different approach to therapy for men. Dannys journey ends with an emotional day at a Jungian male retreat, where like-minded men are trying to figure out how men can evolve their masculinity, rather than abandon it, in the search for a happy future for men

programme.title: Out There
programme.desc: Nathan is suspicious of the Land Rover he saw launching the drone, and when it surprisingly leads him to his brothers girlfriend Robyn, he finds Caleb too and tries to sort things out for Johnny the best way he knows how - but only succeeds in further trapping him in the process

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monacos only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanies daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show

programme.title: The Regency: 10 Years On
programme.desc: Exploring the hotel attack that killed David Byrne, ignited a deadly gang war, and the policing and trials that reshaped Irelands crime landscape

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the worlds fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets an 11-year-old meningitis survivor hoping to finally have hands, and a young couple with fertility complications who dream of starting a family. Also taking part are parents whose only wish is for their young son to take his first steps, and a man who, having shed half his body weight, is left with an unsightly physical reminder of his former self

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The reception team face a change of pace as the holiday season ends. But even if things are quiet, Miguel still has his hands full with maintenance work. Mario has a mystery to solve before some VIP guests check in, while a new entertainer finds himself performing on the graveyard day shift, and sets out to prove his worth by taking charge of a bingo game

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week.

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare dishes for family feasts, starting with roast chicken stuffed with orzo and served on a bed of new potatoes and butterbeans, as well as a warming sausage meatballs and dumplings in a cider sauce. For dessert, its a decadent hazelnut brownie with chocolate sauce and toffee popcorn

programme.title: Alex Polizzis Secret Italy
programme.desc: Alex embraces her lifelong love of Italy and travels North to South, taking in the breath-taking cities of Venice, Milan, Rome and the Amalfi Coast, before unearthing hidden tourist gems in Puglia. She begins in Sardinia
